title: Hypothalamic Osmoreceptors and Thirst
buzzwords: specialized neurons sense plasma tonicity
shrinking cells trigger behavioral drive to drink

baroreceptor input adds non-osmotic stimulus
supraoptic and paraventricular nuclei activated
subfornical organ and OVLT circumventricular sensors
rising tonicity dehydrates hypothalamic cells
osmoreceptors drive thirst above ~290 mOsm/kg

title: Countercurrent Multiplication
buzzwords: kidney separates water from salt along a hairpin
builds a corticomedullary concentration gradient
descending limb water-permeable, ascending impermeable
diluting segment exits at ~100 mOsm/kg

vasa recta exchange preserves the gradient
loop diuretics collapse the medullary gradient
inner medulla reaches ~1,200 mOsm/kg

title: ADH/AQP2 Collecting Duct Axis

acts on principal cells of the collecting duct

V2 receptor signaling inserts aquaporin-2

title: Central Diabetes Insipidus
buzzwords: dilute polyuria with rising serum sodium
intense thirst with preference for cold water
follows pituitary surgery, trauma, or tumor
low urine osmolality despite plasma hypertonicity
water deprivation fails to concentrate urine
hypothalamic-posterior pituitary hormone deficiency
desmopressin sharply raises urine osmolality
ADH deficiency corrected by exogenous vasopressin

title: Nephrogenic Diabetes Insipidus
buzzwords: polyuria unresponsive to hormonal correction
hypernatremia with inappropriately dilute urine
lithium and hypercalcemia are classic triggers
collecting duct resistant despite high ADH levels
water deprivation fails to concentrate urine
desmopressin challenge produces no response
V2 receptor or AQP2 gene mutations in inherited form
kidney unresponsive to vasopressin

title: SIADH
buzzwords: euvolemic hyponatremia with concentrated urine
low plasma osmolality fails to suppress the hormone
urine sodium remains inappropriately high
small cell lung cancer and CNS lesions trigger it
SSRIs and carbamazepine are common culprits
water restriction is first-line therapy
tolvaptan blocks V2 receptors as treatment
inappropriate ADH secretion despite hypotonicity
